Name: Venusaur-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Venusaur-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Weedle
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [G] Pokémon, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

Name: Kakuna
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.
Flavor-Text: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. If all of them are heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has three poisonous stingers on its forelegs and its tail. They are used to jab its enemy repeatedly.

Name: Ledyba
Flavor-Text: When the weather turns cold, lots of Ledyba gather from everywhere to cluster and keep each other warm.

Name: Ledian
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When the stars flicker in the night sky, it flutters about, scattering a glowing powder.

Name: Volbeat
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It communicates with others by lighting up its rear at night. It loves Illumise’s sweet aroma.

Name: Illumise
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its fragrance attracts a swarm of Volbeat, so they draw over 200 patterns in the night sky.

Name: Pansage
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are known to relieve stress.

Name: Simisage
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It attacks enemies with strikes of its thorn-covered tail. This Pokémon is wild tempered.

Name: Chespin
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

Name: Quilladin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It relies on its sturdy shell to deflect predators’ attacks. It counterattacks with its sharp quills.

Name: Chesnaught
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its Tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body.

Name: Scatterbug
Flavor-Text: When under attack from bird Pokémon, it spews a poisonous black powder that causes paralysis on contact.

Name: Spewpa
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it quickly bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them.

Name: Vivillon
Effects: Choose either Asleep or Poisoned. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.
This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Vivillon with many different patterns are found all over the world. These patterns are affected by the climate of their habitat.

Name: Skiddo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition.

Name: Gogoat
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Supporter cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It can tell how its trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathetic sense lets them run as if one being.

Name: Slugma
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

Name: Magcargo
Effects: You may discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a [R] Energy card, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

Name: Pansear
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Very intelligent, it roasts berries before eating them. It likes to help people.

Name: Simisear
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A flame burns inside its body. It scatters embers from its head and tail to sear its opponents.

Name: Fennekin
Flavor-Text: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Braixen
Effects: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a twig stuck in its tail. With friction from its tail fur, it sets the twig on fire and launches into battle.

Name: Delphox
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It gazes into the flame at the tip of its branch to achieve a focused state, which allows it to see into the future.

Name: Fletchinder
Effects: Search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass.

Name: Talonflame
Effects: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.
Discard all [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In the fever of an exciting battle, it showers embers from the gaps between its feathers and takes to the air.

Name: Blastoise-EX
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Blastoise-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shellder
Flavor-Text: Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.

Name: Cloyster
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Flip 5 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.

Name: Staryu
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Even if its body is torn, it can regenerate as long as the glowing central core remains intact.

Name: Starmie
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.
If this Pokémon has any [P] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value the core as a gem.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, attach a [W] Energy card from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They have gentle hearts. Because they rarely fight, many have been caught. Their number has dwindled.

Name: Corsola
Effects: Heal 30 damage and remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It continuously sheds and grows. The tip of its head is prized as a treasure because of its beauty.

Name: Panpour
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. It waters plants with it using its tail.

Name: Simipour
Effects: Put a card from your discard pile on top of your deck.
Flavor-Text: The high-pressure water expelled from its tail is so powerful, it can destroy a concrete wall.

Name: Froakie
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

Name: Frogadier
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

Name: Greninja
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [W] Energy card from your hand. If you do, put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

Name: Raichu
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the number of your Benched Pokémon.
Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When its electricity builds, its muscles are stimulated, and it becomes more aggressive than usual.

Name: Voltorb
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, flip a coin. If heads, put 5 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It was discovered when Poké Balls were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.

Name: Electrode
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It explodes in response to even minor stimuli. It is feared, with the nickname of “The Bomb Ball.”

Name: Emolga-EX
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward. If you attached Energy in this way, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 30 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ekans
Flavor-Text: The older it gets, the longer it grows. At night, it wraps its long body around tree branches to rest.

Name: Arbok
Effects: The Defending Pokémon has no Abilities until the end of your next turn.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The pattern on its belly appears to be a frightening face. Weak foes will flee just at the sight of the pattern.

Name: Spoink
Flavor-Text: It bounces constantly, using its tail like a spring. The shock of bouncing keeps its heart beating.

Name: Grumpig
Effects: You may move an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does a strange dance to control foes’ minds.

Name: Venipede
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poison.
Flavor-Text: It discovers what is going on around it by using the feelers on its head and tail. It is brutally aggressive.

Name: Whirlipede
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

Name: Scolipede
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. That Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It clasps its prey with the claws on its neck until it stops moving. Then it finishes it off with deadly poison.

Name: Phantump
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are created when spirits possess rotten tree stumps. They prefer to live in abandoned forests.

Name: Trevenant
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can’t play any Item cards from his or her hand.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It can control trees at will. It will trap people who harm the forest, so they can never leave.

Name: Pumpkaboo
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active.

Name: Gourgeist
Effects: Put 2 damage counters each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Put damage counters on both Active Pokémon until the remaining HP of each Pokémon is 10.
Flavor-Text: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

Name: Diglett
Effects: Look at the top card of your opponent’s deck. Then, you may have your opponent shuffle his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: Lives about one yard underground where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: In battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.

Name: Rhyhorn
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a [F] Energy, attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging Tackles.

Name: Rhydon
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Rhyperior
Effects: Flip a coin for each [F] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance. This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: From holes in its palms, it fires out Geodude. Its carapace can withstand volcanic eruptions.

Name: Lunatone
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.

Name: Solrock
Effects: If Lunatone is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.

Name: Timburr
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon appear at building sites and help out with construction. They always carry squared logs.

Name: Gurdurr
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: With strengthened bodies, they skillfully wield steel beams to take down buildings.

Name: Conkeldurr
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage. Then, remove all Special Conditions from that Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage and your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It is thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

Name: Sandile
Flavor-Text: It moves along below the sand’s surface, except for its nose and eyes. A dark membrane shields its eyes from the sun.

Name: Krokorok
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

Name: Krookodile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.
Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Very violent Pokémon, they try to clamp down on anything that moves in front of their eyes.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It changes so it looks like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

Name: Inkay
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, (before your attack), if this Pokémon is Confused, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight.

Name: Inkay
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight.

Name: Malamar
Effects: Your opponent flips 4 coins. For each tails, he or she discards a card from his or her hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

Name: Malamar
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: Attach a [D] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: When this legendary Pokémon’s wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of all living creatures.

Name: Yveltal-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Skarmory-EX
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pawniard
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Blades comprise this Pokémon’s entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Metal Wallop attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off.

Name: Honedge
Flavor-Text: Apparently this Pokémon is born when a departed spirit inhabits a sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force.

Name: Doublade
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When Honedge evolves, it divides into two swords, which cooperate via telepathy to coordinate attacks and slash their enemies to ribbons.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch this Pokémon with an Aegislash in your hand. (Any cards attached to this Pokémon, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.)
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Generations of kings were attended by these Pokémon, which used their spectral power to manipulate and control people and Pokémon.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch this Pokémon with an Aegislash in your hand. (Any cards attached to this Pokémon, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.)
Effects: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn. This Pokémon can’t use King’s Shield during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Generations of kings were attended by these Pokémon, which used their spectral power to manipulate and control people and Pokémon.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: Discard a [D] Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It captivates foes with its huge, round eyes, then lulls them to sleep by singing a soothing melody.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It captivates foes with its huge, round eyes, then lulls them to sleep by singing a soothing melody.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Their fur feels so good that if two of them snuggle together, they won’t want to be separated.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Their fur feels so good that if two of them snuggle together, they won’t want to be separated.

Name: Mr. Mime
Effects: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Emanations from its fingertips solidify the air into invisible walls that repel even harsh attacks.

Name: Spritzee
Effects: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It emits a scent that enraptures those who smell it. This fragrance changes depending on what it has eaten.

Name: Aromatisse
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [Y] Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It devises various scents, pleasant and unpleasant, and emits scents that its enemies dislike in order to gain an edge in battle.

Name: Swirlix
Flavor-Text: To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and as sticky as cotton candy.

Name: Slurpuff
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [Y] Energy attached to it can’t be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.)
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a [Y] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

Name: Xerneas-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use X Blast during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Doduo
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.

Name: Dodrio
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads—even for a second—will get pecked severely.

Name: Tauros
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flip a coin for each damage counter on this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: After heightening its will to fight by whipping itself with its three tails, it charges at full speed.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When spotted, this Pokémon escapes backwards by furiously boring into the ground with its tail.

Name: Taillow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a gutsy spirit that makes it bravely take on tough foes. It flies in search of warm climates.

Name: Swellow
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If its two tail feathers are standing at attention, it is proof of good health. It soars elegantly in the sky.

Name: Skitty
Effects: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can’t stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

Name: Delcatty
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 different types of basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It dislikes dirty places. It often searches for a comfortable place in which to groom itself.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

Name: Bibarel
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
You may do 30 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It busily makes its nest withs stacks of branches and roots it has cut up with its sharp incisors.

Name: Lillipup
Flavor-Text: The long hair around its face provides an amazing radar that lets it sense subtle changes in its surroundings.

Name: Herdier
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This very loyal Pokémon helps Trainers, and it also takes care of other Pokémon.

Name: Stoutland
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage.
Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

Name: Bunnelby
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: Put 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: With their powerful ears, they can heft boulders of a ton or more with ease. They can be a big help at construction sites.

Name: Fletchling
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

Name: Cassius
Description: Shuffle 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Evosoda
Description: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Fairy Garden
Description: Each Pokémon that has any [Y] Energy attached to it (both yours and your opponent’s) has no Retreat Cost.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Great Ball
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Hard Charm
Description: Any damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Max Revive
Description: Put a Pokémon from your discard pile on top of your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Muscle Band
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Professor Sycamore
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Professor’s Letter
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Red Card
Description: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Roller Skates
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, draw 3 cards.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Shadow Circle
Description: Each Pokémon that has any [D] Energy attached to it (both yours and your opponent’s) has no Weakness.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Shauna
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Super Potion
Description: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Team Flare Grunt
Description: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rainbow Energy
Description: This card provides [C] Energy. While in play, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.

Name: Venusaur-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blastoise-EX
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Emolga-EX
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward. If you attached Energy in this way, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 30 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Yveltal-EX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Skarmory-EX
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 40 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Xerneas-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use X Blast during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
